_note: Emergency/standby/interconnect (Arts 700-705) bundled in art700-705-emergency-standby.json. Other Ch 7 articles inline.

articles.scope: Stationary ESS — batteries (Li-ion, lead-acid, flow), capacitors. Listing per UL 9540 (706.4), disconnect (706.7), location (residential garage, utility closet limits), interconnection with PV and grid (705).

key_sections.title: Listing
key_sections.summary: ESS must be listed per UL 9540 (System) and components per UL 9540A (Thermal runaway test)

key_sections.title: Disconnecting Means
key_sections.summary: Disconnect within sight or remote with lockout-tagout; identified for service personnel; first responder readability

key_sections.title: Qualified Personnel
key_sections.summary: Installation by qualified persons; commissioning per manufacturer

key_sections.title: Self-Contained Energy Storage System
key_sections.summary: Listed self-contained ESS — installation, ventilation, location restrictions

common_errors: Treating an ESS as just an Art 480 battery installation — 706 layered requirements apply, including UL 9540 listing
Installing residential Li-ion ESS in an attached garage without complying with NFPA 855 separation rules (referenced from 706)
articles.divergence_notes: Art 706 (since NEC 2017) consolidates ESS-specific rules; IEC handles via IEC 62933 (technical) + IEC 60364-8-2 (installation). UL 9540 listing is NEC-mandated; IEC equivalent is IEC 62933-5-2. NFPA 855 (Stationary Energy Storage Systems) is the companion installation standard referenced from Art 706; IEC equivalent IEC 62933-5-1.
articles.usage_notes: For PV+ESS, read Art 690 + 705 + 706 together. ESS rapid shutdown handled via 690.12 when PV-coupled.

articles.scope: Off-grid power systems — PV-only, wind-only, gen-only, or hybrid where no utility connection exists. Voltage limits, backup load sizing, identification of power sources.

key_sections.title: Identification of Power Sources
key_sections.summary: Permanent plaque at service equipment identifying all power sources

key_sections.title: General
key_sections.summary: Backup load may be lower than original-utility load; sized per actual standalone capacity

common_errors: Sizing stand-alone system to original utility load instead of actual standalone autonomy needs
articles.divergence_notes: Art 710 is NEC-specific to stand-alone (no utility) systems. IEC 60364-8-2 covers prosumer (with-grid) systems more thoroughly; standalone is via national supplements.
articles.usage_notes: Document the stand-alone capacity vs total load on the design narrative; cite NEC 710 for the source identification plaque requirement.

articles.scope: DC microgrids — distribution at DC voltage levels, bus voltages, interconnection with AC system, DC OCPDs.

key_sections.title: Voltage
key_sections.summary: DC microgrid bus voltage classifications: ≤60 V (ELV), 60-1500 V

common_errors: Using AC-rated OCPDs in a DC microgrid — DC has no zero-crossing; OCPDs must be DC-rated
articles.divergence_notes: Art 712 is NEC-specific. IEC handles DC distribution via IEC 60364-5-52 with DC-specific Annex content; full DC microgrid standardisation is emerging (IEC 60364-8-3 in development).
articles.usage_notes: DC microgrid design is still emerging; coordinate with the equipment vendor's DC-rated component list.

articles.article_title: Class 1, Class 2, and Class 3 Remote-Control, Signaling, and Power-Limited Circuits
articles.scope: Low-voltage / power-limited circuits — Class 1 (≤30 V, 1000 VA limited), Class 2 (60 V, 100 VA), Class 3 (similar voltage, lower energy). Wiring methods, separation from other circuits.

key_sections.title: Power Sources for Class 2 and Class 3 Circuits
key_sections.summary: Listed Class 2 / 3 transformer or power supply per UL 1310

key_sections.title: Conductors of Different Circuits in Same Cable, Cable Tray, Enclosure, or Raceway
key_sections.summary: Class 2/3 circuits cannot share with Class 1, power, or lighting circuits except with permitted separation

common_errors: Mixing Class 2 control wiring with power circuits in the same raceway — prohibited
articles.divergence_notes: NEC Class 2/3 ≈ IEC SELV/PELV but with different voltage/energy limits and listing regimes.
articles.usage_notes: Specify the Class on every low-voltage circuit on the schedule.

articles.scope: Fire alarm circuits — Non-power-limited (NPLFA) and Power-limited (PLFA) systems. Wiring methods, conductor sizing, separation from other circuits, cable listing (FPLP, FPL, FPLR).

key_sections.title: Power Sources for Power-Limited Fire Alarm Circuits
key_sections.summary: Listed PLFA transformer or power supply

key_sections.title: Listing and Marking of PLFA Cables

common_errors: Using non-fire-rated cable on plenum-rated fire alarm circuit — FPLP required in air-handling spaces
articles.divergence_notes: Art 760 is the NEC-specific fire alarm wiring standard; NFPA 72 (National Fire Alarm Code) is the companion device-and-system standard. IEC covers fire alarm wiring via 60364-5-56 + national supplements.
articles.usage_notes: Coordinate with the project's NFPA 72 fire alarm design. Cable type (FPLP/FPLR/FPL) by installation location.

articles.scope: Optical fiber cable installation — conductive vs non-conductive cable types, abandoned cable removal, raceway and routing rules.

key_sections.title: Listing and Marking of Optical Fiber Cables

common_errors: Leaving abandoned fiber in place after retrofit — 770.25 requires removal
articles.divergence_notes: Art 770 (optical fiber) has no direct IEC 60364 analogue; IEC defers to ISO/IEC 11801 (premises cabling).
articles.usage_notes: Coordinate with the structured cabling design (ISO/IEC 11801).

articles.article_title: Emergency Systems / Legally Required Standby / Optional Standby / Interconnect (Bundle 700, 701, 702, 705)
articles.scope: Emergency systems, legally required standby, optional standby, interconnected power production sources.
